name: GDPR consent events destructured without Zod parse
description: [FCR 2026-06-21 · L8-02] app/consent.requested and app/consent.revoked destructured with no schema; a malformed/replayed revokedAt makes the revocation-current guard pass vacuously on a cascade-delete path. Fix: add schemas + safeParse with NonRetriableError.
acceptanceCriteria:
Root cause: apps/api/src/inngest/functions/consent-revocation.ts:58 and consent-reminders.ts:32–36 destructure event.data with manual typeof guards instead of Zod schemas. The revocation-current guard functions (consent.ts:1044, consent-v2.ts:977) both short-circuit `return true` when revokedAt is falsy — treating a missing or null timestamp as generation-confirmed — which unconditionally authorizes cascade child-profile deletion on any replayed or malformed app/consent.revoked event.

Variants: (a) consent.revoked — revokedAt null (DB responded_at IS NULL on the legacy path) makes isConsentRevocationGenerationCurrent() return true, bypassing the generation check and authorizing deletion; (b) consent.revoked — revokedAt field absent from payload (missing field) produces same undefined path; (c) consent.revoked — revokedAt is an invalid ISO string, converts to NaN Date then undefined, same vacuous pass; (d) consent.revoked — idempotency key collapses on null ('childProfileId-null'), allowing duplicate execution alongside a valid-keyed re-fire, both reaching the deletion path; (e) consent.requested — no schema, malformed requestedAt silently skips the day-7/14/25 email sequence and auto-delete guard on the reminder path.

Fix: add Zod schemas for both event payloads; safeParse at the top of each function and throw NonRetriableError on failure. Fix both guard functions to return false (not true) when revokedAt is falsy — missing timestamp means cannot confirm generation, do not proceed.

Regression test: in apps/api/src/inngest/functions/consent-revocation.test.ts (or similar integration test), fire a consent.revoked event with revokedAt omitted; assert the Inngest function throws NonRetriableError and does NOT call deleteProfileIfConsentWithdrawn or deletePersonIfConsentWithdrawnV2. Must fail without the fix (function proceeds to deletion) and pass with it (NonRetriableError thrown). Run: `pnpm exec nx run api:test --testPathPattern=consent-revocation`.
riskImpact: GDPR deletion-path guard defeated by a malformed payload.
